architectural_summary:
## Architectural Summary

**Overall Architecture Pattern**: This is a monolithic backend application (PocketBase) built as a single executable with embedded SQLite. The structure follows a layered architecture with clear separation between API handlers, core business logic, and data access.

**Tech Stack**: Built in Go with SQLite (`modernc.org/sqlite`) as the primary database. Uses `github.com/spf13/cobra` for CLI, `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5` for authentication, and `github.com/pocketbase/dbx` for database abstraction. External dependencies include imaging (`github.com/disintegration/imaging`), email (`github.com/domodwyer/mailyak/v3`), and OAuth2 (`golang.org/x/oauth2`).

**API Surface**: RESTful API with numerous endpoints under `/apis/` covering auth (password, OAuth2, OTP), CRUD operations (`record_crud.go`), file handling, backups, logs, and realtime capabilities (`realtime.go`). Middleware includes CORS, rate limiting, and gzip compression.

**Data Layer**: SQLite with custom ORM-like abstractions in `/core/`. Key models include `collection_model.go`, `record_model.go`, and supporting models for auth (`auth_origin_model.go`), MFA, OTP, and logs. Field types are extensible (text, number, relation, file, etc.) with validation via `github.com/go-ozzo/ozzo-validation/v4`.

**Infrastructure Maturity**: CI/CD via GitHub Actions (implied by `"ci_cd": "github-actions"`). No containerization or cloud provider specified in infrastructure; deployment appears to be via standalone binary. Includes migration system (`migrations_list.go`, `migrations_runner.go`) and backup utilities.

**Strengths**: Well-structured separation of concerns—APIs, core logic, and data models are in distinct directories. Comprehensive test coverage (many `*_test.go` files). Built-in auth flows (email, OAuth2, MFA) and realtime features. Validation and field system is modular and extensible.

**Weaknesses / Gaps**: Lack of containerization (e.g., Docker) limits deployment flexibility. No built-in support for other databases besides SQLite. Infrastructure details (caching, message queues) are absent. Monolithic design may hinder scalability for high-load scenarios.
